data.title: Whale Wallet Awakens After 500 Days, Withdraws 4,700 ETH
data.description: According to PANews, a whale wallet has become active after approximately 500 days of dormancy, withdrawing 4,700 ETH, valued at $12.98 million, from an exchange. This significant movement was detected by Lookonchain monitoring services.

data.title: BlackRock Experiences Significant Outflow in IBIT Holdings
data.description: According to Odaily, recent monitoring by Trader T revealed that BlackRock's IBIT experienced a net outflow of $111.88 million yesterday.

data.title: ACCC Warns Of Increased Scam Risks Amid Potential Crypto Regulation Changes
data.description: According to Foresight News, the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) Chair, Gina Cass-Gottlieb, has expressed concerns that U.S. President Donald Trump's promise to ease cryptocurrency regulations could make consumers more vulnerable to investment scams. Cass-Gottlieb highlighted the complexity of global crime and the potential regulatory relaxation as factors contributing to this risk. The ACCC plans to intensify its focus on these issues. Data from the ACCC reveals that in 2023, Australian consumers lost over 1.3 billion AUD to investment scams, with a significant portion involving cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Ethereum Developers Discuss Pectra Upgrade And EIP Finalization
data.description: According to Foresight News, Christine Kim, Vice President of Research at Galaxy, summarized the 151st Ethereum Core Developers Consensus (ACDC) call. This week, developers discussed the client teams' preparations for the Pectra upgrade on the Holesky testnet and the next steps for PeerDAS Devnet 5. Developers also agreed to finalize EIP 7872, which concerns the maximum blob flag for local builders, and EIP 7870, which provides hardware and bandwidth recommendations for validators and full nodes. Additionally, EF test engineer Mario Vega confirmed that all Pectra system contracts have been deployed on Holesky, Sepolia, and the Ethereum mainnet.

data.title: BlackRock IBIT saw a net outflow of $111.88 million yesterday
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reported that according to Trader T monitoring, BlackRock IBIT had a net outflow of US$111.88 million yesterday.

data.title: 151st Ethereum ACDC meeting: developers agree to finalize EIP 7872 and EIP 7870
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Christine Kim, Vice President of Galaxy Research, published an article summarizing the 151st Ethereum Core Developer Consensus (ACDC) conference call. This week, developers discussed the client team's preparations for the Pectra upgrade on the Holesky testnet and the next steps for PeerDAS Devnet 5.
Developers also agreed to finalize EIP 7872 (maximum blob flag for local builders) and EIP 7870 (hardware and bandwidth recommendations for validators and full nodes); in addition, EF test engineer Mario Vega said that all Pectra system contracts have been deployed to Holesky, Sepolia, and the Ethereum mainnet.

data.title: Infinity Ground Completes $2 Million Seed Round
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Infinity Ground announced the completion of a $2 million seed round of financing, with participation from Animoca Brands, MARBLEX, MH Ventures, Frachtis Ventures, KuCoin Ventures, KnightFury, Presto Labs and Paka.
Infinity Ground was selected for the MVB accelerator jointly organized by YZi Labs, BNB Chain and CoinMarketCap. The project plans to help Web3 creators build, deploy and monetize AI-driven applications "Text to App" without code through its innovative Agentic IDE (intelligent development environment) and IDK (intelligent development toolkit).

data.title: Only a third of Australians think Trump's presidency would be good for cryptocurrencies
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News After Trump was elected as the US President, the cryptocurrency market soared due to his promise to support the industry, but a recent survey shows that only about a third of Australians think he is optimistic about cryptocurrencies.
A survey of 2,100 local adults released by Australian cryptocurrency exchange Independent Reserve on February 21 found that 31% of people think Trump is good for cryptocurrencies, while 8% think he is bad for the industry. Most respondents (about 60%) are neutral on the topic.
The survey found that cryptocurrency investors have a more positive view of Trump than non-investors. Half of Australian cryptocurrency investors said he is positive about cryptocurrencies, while 44% are neutral. Only 6% said he is negative about the cryptocurrency industry. Among non-cryptocurrency investors, only about 20% said he is positive about cryptocurrencies, while 10% think he is negative for the industry. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Japan's core inflation hits 19-month high, reinforcing expectations of a rate hike by the central bank
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Data released earlier today showed that Japan's core consumer inflation accelerated in January to 3.2% year-on-year, the fastest growth in 19 months. The stronger-than-expected growth reinforced expectations that the Bank of Japan may continue to raise interest rates as inflationary pressures persist. The price increase appears to be in line with the central bank's forecast. Analysts pointed out that service industry inflation did not accelerate, rising 1.4% year-on-year in January, compared with 1.6% in December, but overall, commodity inflation did not slow down. Japan's inflation rate has been higher than the central bank's 2% target for nearly three years. This has prompted a shift in monetary policy. Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda previously hinted that the central bank may raise interest rates further if wage growth continues to support consumption, enabling companies to raise wages and keep prices rising. (Jinshi)

data.title: Japan's Core Consumer Inflation Reaches 19-Month High In January
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Japan's core consumer inflation accelerated in January, reaching 3.2% year-on-year, marking the fastest pace in 19 months. This stronger-than-expected growth has reinforced expectations that the Bank of Japan may continue to raise interest rates as inflationary pressures persist. The rise in prices aligns with the central bank's forecasts. Analysts noted that while inflation in the services sector did not accelerate, increasing by 1.4% year-on-year in January compared to 1.6% in December, overall goods inflation has not slowed. For nearly three years, Japan's inflation rate has remained above the central bank's 2% target, prompting a shift in monetary policy. Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda previously indicated that if wage growth continues to support consumption, enabling companies to raise wages and maintain price increases, the central bank might further increase interest rates.
